title: linaria alpina subsp. petraea
intro: Linaria alpina subsp. petraea, also known as the alpine toadflax or alpine linaria, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia, where it is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Linaria alpina subsp. petraea are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote overall well-being and provide relief from various health issues.
